prompt:
Incident reviews here are miserable because every box logs in its own dialect and I end up eyeballing three terminals. Write me logmerge.py: point it at N log files and get back ONE list, ordered by time. The fleet writes exactly three shapes, and a given file only ever contains one of them, so sniff the format per file from its first non-blank line: ISO like `2026-03-14T09:26:53 web-1 GET /api/users 200` (optional .fractional seconds, 1-6 digits), plain epoch seconds like `1773480410 worker-3 job 87 picked up` (optional fraction), and syslog style like `Mar 14 09:26:53 gate-1 sshd[411]: opened` (three-letter month, day either zero- or space-padded like `Mar  4`). Once sniffed, every later non-blank line in that file MUST parse the same way or you raise ValueError telling me which file and which line (put the path and `line N` in the message). Same if the first line matches nothing.

API, all in logmerge.py: `sniff_format(line)` -> 'iso' | 'syslog' | 'epoch' (ValueError otherwise). `parse_timestamp(line, fmt, assume_year=2026)` -> UTC epoch seconds as a float; naive ISO and syslog stamps are UTC by fleet policy, and syslog lines (no year) get assume_year, every one of them, no rollover guessing. `merge_files(paths, skew=0.0, assume_year=2026)` -> list of dicts {'ts': float, 'source': the path exactly as given, 'lineno': physical 1-based line number in that file, 'line': the original line with only its trailing newline stripped}. Blank/whitespace-only lines are skipped but still count for numbering. Empty files are fine and contribute nothing.

The part I actually care about, because our writers batch-flush: files are only ~sorted. `skew` is the tolerance in seconds -- a line may lag at most `skew` behind the furthest-ahead timestamp already seen IN THAT FILE and still be quietly re-sorted into its correct global position; anything further behind means the file is genuinely corrupt and you raise ValueError with the path and `line N`. Default skew=0.0: equal stamps fine, any regression fatal. Negative skew is a ValueError.

Output ordering, exactly: ascending ts; ties broken by the file's position in the `paths` argument (earlier list position wins), then by line number. That tie-break is load-bearing -- downstream diffing depends on it being stable.

Contract: `python3 test_logmerge.py` (it writes its own fixture files, all timestamps fixed). Don't touch the test file. Stdlib only.
